system_description: Swahili Bantu noun-class agreement. 15-18 noun classes (genders) control prefix agreement on verbs, adjectives, and relative pronouns simultaneously. Subject agreement prefix and object agreement prefix both appear on the verb: a-na-ki-soma (3sg.SUBJ-PRES-7.OBJ-read = he reads it). This creates a dual-channel agreement system where the verb alone identifies both subject and object class. Case is not morphologically marked on nouns; disambiguation relies entirely on agreement and rigid SVO order.
notes: PRE-REGISTERED prediction: k_eff ~1.8-2.6. Swahili has the richest agreement system in this suite (noun class agreement on both subject and object simultaneously), but NO case marking. Prediction: agreement will dominate but not as sharply as case-dominant languages, because two agreement channels compete rather than one system monopolizing. Falsifier: does dual-channel noun-class agreement produce measurably lower k_eff than single-channel agreement (Romance languages)?
